content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6

## Summary

The amendment identifies a genuine correctness defect where shared acceptance ownership enrichment in build_planned_elsewhere_context trusts implicit plan ID mappings even when membership_confirmed=false. The acceptance_map loop (lines 1357-1381) matches the live bead ID against canonicalized plan refs without verifying that the identity mapping is reliable, leading to incorrect 'planned elsewhere' context when the bead was only matched by title fallback. The existing location_hint gate at line 1430 already demonstrates the correct pattern — the same guard should extend to acceptance-derived enrichment.

## Votes

- `fr-14-1b5a8e5d`: Accept
  The defect is real and reproducible. When membership_confirmed=false, the live bead's ID (e.g. ms-alpha.bead-2) is not reliably mapped to the plan's positional bead-2 slot, yet the acceptance_map loop at lines 1357-1381 trusts this mapping unconditionally via canonicalize_bundle_bead_ref. This causes incorrect shared acceptance ownership entries to appear in the prompt (e.g. claiming bead-4 shares acceptance with the current bead when the plan mapping is explicitly unconfirmed). The code already recognizes this problem for neighbor inference at line 1430 but fails to apply the same guard earlier. The fix — gating plan-derived acceptance enrichment on confirmed membership — is consistent with how the rest of the function handles unconfirmed state, and the regression test for renamed-live-bead/shared-acceptance is needed since the existing test at line 2561 only covers the confirmed path.
